source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning KMS Related Roles to Users

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning KMS Related Roles to Users
input_row.description: It is recommended that the principle of 'Separation of Duties' is enforced while assigning KMS related roles to users.
input_row.rationale: The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS Admin allows the user/identity to create, delete, and manage service account(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to encrypt and decrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to encrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to decrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). Separation of duties is the concept of ensuring that one individual does not have all necessary permissions to be able to complete a malicious action. In Cloud KMS, this could be an action such as using a key to access and decrypt data a user should not normally have access to. Separation of duties is a business control typically used in larger organizations, meant to help avoid security or privacy incidents and errors. It is considered best practice. No user(s) should have Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter roles assigned at the same time. Impact: Removed roles should be assigned to another user based on business needs.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam 2. Ensure no member has the roles Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter assigned. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all users and role assignments: gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID 2. Ensure that there are no common users found in the member section for roles cloudkms.admin and any one of Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam 2. For any member having Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter roles granted/assigned, click the Delete Bin icon to remove the role from the member. Note: Removing a role should be done based on the business requirement. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/separation-of-duties Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Cloud KMS Admin and Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter. To avoid misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to a very limited group of users. Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal.
input_row.impact: Removed roles should be assigned to another user based on business needs.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/separation-of-duties Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Cloud KMS Admin and Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter. To avoid misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to a very limited group of users. Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control can be verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, focusing on IAM roles assigned to users. This involves checking the separation of duties regarding the roles associated with Cloud KMS. Since IAM roles and their assignments are standard properties that can be queried through GCP APIs and there are explicit instructions in the audit field that describe how the verification can be performed using the `gcloud` command, the audit approach is determined to be Automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_kms_iam_role_separation_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API calls/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK methods to use:** Use the `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` command to retrieve the IAM policy of the specified project, which includes all users and their role assignments.
  
- **GCP CLI commands (if applicable):** 
  - Execute `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` to get the IAM policy details for the project.
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:** IAM roles and their assignments within the project, focusing on roles related to Cloud KMS.
  
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:** 
  - Ensure no user simultaneously has the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the following roles:
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyDecrypter`
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** No users have both the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter`, `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter`, or `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter` roles assigned.
  - **Fail:** At least one user has both the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and one or more of the specified `Cloud KMS` roles assigned.

This automated procedure will effectively ensure compliance with the control by verifying role assignments in a project to enforce the separation of duties in line with CIS recommendations.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **PARTIALLY_AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The control is classified as "Automated," which is partially correct. The retrieval of IAM policy assignments and detection of users with conflicting KMS roles **can** be fully automated using GCP APIs or `gcloud` commands. However, the **interpretation of "separation of duties"** is context-dependent and may require human judgment (e.g., service accounts, group memberships, or business justifications). The technical check for role overlap is automatable, but full compliance assessment may need manual review for context.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Capability:**  
  Yes, GCP IAM policies can be queried via `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` or the [projects.getIamPolicy API](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects/getIamPolicy). Parsing and checking for users with both admin and encrypter/decrypter roles is feasible via automation.
- **Other Considerations:**  
  GCP does not have a native "Config" service like AWS Config for continuous compliance, but automation via scripts or Security Command Center custom modules is possible.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The use of `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` is correct for project-level IAM. For org/folder-level roles, `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy` or `gcloud folders get-iam-policy` may also be relevant.
  - The role list is accurate for KMS admin and crypto key roles.
  - The pass/fail logic is sound for detecting direct assignment overlap.
- **Limitations:**  
  - Does **not account for indirect assignments** (e.g., group membership, custom roles that aggregate permissions, or inherited roles from higher-level resources).
  - Does **not consider service accounts** or automated processes, which may be legitimate exceptions.
- **Program Name:**  
  - `gcp_kms_iam_role_separation_enforced` is descriptive but could be more specific (see Improvements).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Missing Considerations:**
  - **Group Memberships:** Users may receive roles via Google Groups. The script should resolve group memberships to individual users.
  - **Custom Roles:** Custom roles may aggregate both admin and crypto key permissions. The script should parse custom roles' permissions.
  - **Service Accounts:** Should clarify if service accounts are in scope or if exceptions are allowed.
  - **Resource Scope:** Should specify whether only project-level roles are checked or also org/folder-level assignments.
  - **Inherited Roles:** Should check for inherited roles from parent resources.
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**
  - Use [Cloud Asset Inventory](https://cloud.google.com/asset-inventory/docs/reference/rest/v1/assets/searchAllIamPolicies) for organization-wide IAM policy search.
  - Use [IAM Policy Analyzer](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/policy-analyzer) for advanced analysis.
- **Better Program Name Suggestion:**
  - `gcp_kms_iam_separation_of_duties_check`
  - `gcp_kms_iam_conflicting_roles_detected`
  - `gcp_kms_iam_role_overlap_check`
  - Consider including "conflicting_roles" or "separation_of_duties" for clarity.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **MEDIUM**

- **Reason:**  
  The technical approach is fundamentally sound for direct IAM role assignments at the project level. However, the assessment does not address group memberships, custom roles, or inherited roles, which are common in enterprise GCP environments and critical for a robust compliance check.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Action:**  
  - **Expand automation details** to include:
    - Parsing group memberships to resolve all users.
    - Checking custom roles for conflicting permissions.
    - Clarifying treatment of service accounts and inherited roles.
    - Optionally, suggest using Cloud Asset Inventory or IAM Policy Analyzer for broader and more accurate analysis.
  - **Update program name** to be more specific (e.g., `gcp_kms_iam_separation_of_duties_check`).
  - **Document limitations** if full automation is not feasible for all scenarios.
- **Re-classification:**  
  - Keep as "Automated" for the technical check, but **note that some manual review may be required** for contextual interpretation (e.g., business exceptions, service accounts).

---

**Summary Table**

| Section              | Assessment         |
|----------------------|-------------------|
| Approach Validation  | PARTIALLY_AGREE   |
| Technical Accuracy   | 8/10              |
| Improvements         | See above         |
| Confidence           | MEDIUM            |
| Final Recommendation | Modify            |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
The separation of duties for KMS-related IAM roles can be fully audited using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). All necessary IAM role assignments are exposed via the `get-iam-policy` API for projects, folders, and organizations. The technical check—ensuring no user has both admin and encrypter/decrypter roles—can be programmatically enforced without requiring human judgment for the core compliance check. While business context may be relevant for remediation, the audit itself is entirely automatable.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_kms_iam_no_dual_admin_encrypter_roles

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: IAM roles for Cloud KMS (Key Management Service)
- API/CLI Method:  
  - `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID`  
  - (Optionally: `gcloud folders get-iam-policy FOLDER_ID`, `gcloud organizations get-iam-policy ORG_ID` for broader scope)
- Properties to Check:  
  - Members assigned to `roles/cloudkms.admin`
  - Members assigned to any of:  
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyDecrypter`
  - Identify any member (user, group, or service account) present in both the admin and any encrypter/decrypter role lists.
- Pass Criteria:  
  - No member is assigned both `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the encrypter/decrypter roles listed above.
- Fail Criteria:  
  - Any member is found to have both `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the encrypter/decrypter roles.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
While the second opinion noted that business context may require manual review for exceptions, the audit step itself—detecting overlapping role assignments—is fully automatable using GCP APIs. Remediation or exception handling may require human input, but the compliance check does not.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
